primary_text:
Rachel Ward spent years watching her Kempsey cattle farm lose money selling to supermarkets. After the 2020 bushfires, she rebuilt the whole operation as regenerative.

Now her beef reaches your kitchen in 2-3 days. Not 14-21 days in cold storage.

FarmThru exists to make stories like Rachel's the norm, not the exception. 50+ farms. Zero middlemen. Real food, direct to your door.

$5 refundable deposit secures VIP access when the Birchal campaign opens in July.

Not financial advice.
headline: From Bushfire to Your Table
description: Rachel rebuilt her farm as regenerative. Now her beef goes direct to Sydney families via FarmThru.
creative_brief: Lead with Rachel's Farm transformation as emotional proof of the FarmThru mission. Bushfire-to-regenerative arc shows real stakes. Transition from her story to the broader movement, then soft investment CTA. Food is the hero.

primary_text:
Own a piece of the future of food.

FarmThru is opening equity crowdfunding on Birchal this July. The families who buy from us can now invest in us.

From $50. Cap at $10,000 per investor.

We deliver regenerative meat, dairy and seafood from 50+ farms to 2,000+ Sydney households. Now we grow with the people who believe in this.

$5 refundable deposit locks in VIP access.

Not financial advice. Read the disclosure document on Birchal before investing.
headline: Own a Piece of the Future of Food
description: Equity crowdfunding on Birchal. Invest from $50. Join 2,000+ families already eating better.
creative_brief: Bold aspiration lead. 'Own a piece of the future of food' is the strongest cause-purpose hook from testing. Stack traction proof (50+ farms, 2,000+ customers) quickly, then investment terms. Full compliance language included.

primary_text:
If your beef travelled 2,400km to reach your fridge, it's not fresh. It's survived.

Supermarket meat sits in cold storage for 14-21 days. By the time you cook it, the best of it is gone.

FarmThru delivers 100% grass-fed, regenerative beef from NSW farms to your door in 2-3 days. No hormones. No feedlots. No middlemen.

50+ farms. Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong.

Taste the difference for yourself.
headline: Your Beef Travelled 2,400km
description: Supermarket beef: 14-21 days cold storage. FarmThru: 2-3 days paddock to door. You decide.
creative_brief: If-then structure creates instant cognitive dissonance. Loss aversion framing (what you're losing by staying with supermarket) is 2x more powerful than gain framing. Specific numbers (2,400km, 14-21 days) make it concrete. Invitation to taste, not pressure to switch.

primary_text:
Here's how your supermarket steak gets to you. Farm in Queensland. Trucked to a wholesaler. Stored in a warehouse. Shipped to a distribution centre. Delivered to the store. Sits on the shelf. 14-21 days later, you buy it.

Here's how FarmThru works. Regenerative farm in NSW. Packed at our Brookvale hub. Delivered to your door. 2-3 days.

Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. 50+ farms, direct.

Only food we'd feed our own.
headline: Two Supply Chains. One Choice.
description: Supermarket: 6 stops, 14-21 days. FarmThru: 3 stops, 2-3 days. From 50+ regenerative farms.
creative_brief: Side-by-side supply chain narrative. Walk the reader through the supermarket chain step by step (each step = more time, more middlemen), then contrast with FarmThru's simplicity. Educational tone, let the comparison do the persuading. Tagline close.

primary_text:
I started FarmThru because my kids deserved better than supermarket meat.

I wanted to know the farmer. I wanted beef that was days old, not weeks old. I wanted animals raised on pasture, not in feedlots.

So I drove to Kempsey, met Rachel, and started delivering her regenerative beef direct to families in Sydney.

That was the beginning. Now we work with 50+ farms and deliver to 2,000+ households across Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong.

Only food we'd feed our own.

Not financial advice. Disclosure document on Birchal.
headline: My Kids Deserved Better
description: Founder-led. 50+ regenerative farms. Delivered direct to Sydney families in 2-3 days.
creative_brief: Founder origin story. Personal motivation (kids, food quality) makes the brand human. Specific details (Kempsey, Rachel) ground the narrative. Scale from personal moment to 50+ farms and 2,000+ customers. Tagline close. No investment CTA here, pure brand building.

primary_text:
I used to buy everything from Woolworths. Meat, dairy, eggs. I never thought about where it came from.

Then I visited a regenerative farm in Kempsey and watched cattle graze open paddocks. No feedlots. No hormones. The farmer knew every animal by name.

I couldn't go back to supermarket beef after that. So I built FarmThru.

50+ farms. 2,000+ families. 2-3 days paddock to door. Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong.

Join the waitlist to see what real food looks like.
headline: I Never Used to Think About My Food
description: From supermarket shopper to building FarmThru. 50+ regenerative farms, delivered in days.
creative_brief: Vulnerability-first founder confession. Start where the audience is (supermarket shopper who doesn't think about provenance), then show the moment of transformation. Relatable entry point, no judgment. Invitation to discover, not pressure to act.

primary_text:
50+ regenerative farms. 2,000+ customers. Delivering across Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong from our Brookvale hub.

FarmThru isn't an idea. It's an operating business with real traction.

We cut out warehouses and wholesalers. Farm to hub to kitchen. Farmers get a fairer price. You get food that's 2-3 days old, not weeks.

Equity crowdfunding on Birchal, July 2026. Invest from $50.

Not financial advice. Read the Birchal disclosure document before investing.

description: 50+ farms, 2,000+ customers, operating hub in Brookvale. Invest from $50 on Birchal.
creative_brief: Traction-first for investment-minded audience. Stack the metrics (farms, customers, geography, hub) to show this is a real business. Honest headline includes 'Real Risk' for balance. Business model clarity (direct supply chain) as proof of unit economics logic.

primary_text:
The Australian grocery market is worth $130B. Two supermarkets control most of it.

FarmThru goes direct. 50+ regenerative farms to your door. No warehouses. No wholesalers. Farmers get a fair price and you get food that's actually fresh.

Direct-to-consumer food is growing 24% year on year. We already have 2,000+ customers across Sydney.

Equity crowdfunding on Birchal. From $50, capped at $10,000.

$5 refundable deposit for VIP access. Not financial advice.
headline: $130B Market. Two Gatekeepers.
description: FarmThru goes direct to your door. 50+ farms, 2,000+ customers. Invest from $50 on Birchal.
creative_brief: Market opportunity frame for sophisticated retail investors. $130B market size and duopoly framing sets up the disruption thesis. Growth data (24% DTC) and traction (2,000+ customers) provide evidence. Still leads with food and farmer benefit, not pure finance.

primary_text:
The first time I cooked a FarmThru steak, I thought I'd been eating cardboard my whole life.

100% grass-fed beef from regenerative farms. No hormones. No feedlots. The animal lived on pasture. You can taste it.

This isn't fancy marketing. It's what happens when beef is 2-3 days old instead of 14-21 days in cold storage.

50+ farms. Delivered across Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong.

Only food we'd feed our own.

description: 100% grass-fed regenerative beef. No hormones, no feedlots. Paddock to door in 2-3 days.
creative_brief: First-person sensory moment creates visceral engagement. Stack the quality credentials (grass-fed, no hormones, pasture-raised) then explain WHY it tastes different with the freshness comparison. Tagline close. No investment CTA, pure food-as-hero.

primary_text:
Supermarket beef: 14-21 days in cold storage. Average journey: 2,400km.

FarmThru beef: 2-3 days paddock to door. From regenerative farms you can actually visit.

100% grass-fed. No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. Animals raised on open pasture the way they should be.

We work with 50+ farms across NSW and deliver to Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong.

Days old, not weeks old. You will taste the difference.

description: Regenerative food from 50+ farms, delivered in days. Sydney, Central Coast, Wollongong.

primary_text:
The 2020 bushfires nearly destroyed Rachel Ward's cattle property in Kempsey. Instead of rebuilding the old way, she went regenerative.

No feedlots. No hormones. Animals on pasture, soil improving every season.

Her beef travels from Kempsey to our Brookvale hub, then to your door. 2-3 days, not weeks.

FarmThru works with 50+ farms like Rachel's. More people should know where their food comes from.

Join the waitlist. $5 refundable deposit for VIP access.

Not financial advice.
headline: She Lost Everything. Then Rebuilt.
description: Rachel's regenerative beef goes from Kempsey to your kitchen in 2-3 days. No middlemen.
creative_brief: Full transformation arc: destruction (bushfires) to rebuilding (regenerative) to outcome (fresh beef direct to families). Rachel's story humanises the supply chain. Specificity on location (Kempsey, Brookvale hub) grounds the narrative. Soft CTA.

primary_text:
"I was losing money on every kilo I sold to the supermarket. They didn't care about the animal, the land, or my family."

That's the reality for thousands of Australian farmers. The system is broken. Farms go under while supermarkets post record profits.

FarmThru cuts out the middlemen entirely. Farm to hub to your kitchen. 50+ regenerative farms paid a fair price.

We're raising on Birchal this July. Invest from $50.

creative_brief: Open with a farmer's voice expressing real frustration with the supermarket system. Agitate the structural problem (farmer margin squeeze), then position FarmThru as the direct alternative. Farmer-as-hero framing, not brand-as-hero.

primary_text:

50+ regenerative farms across NSW. 2,000+ families already ordering every week. A supply chain with zero warehouses and zero wholesalers. Food that's 2-3 days old, not weeks.

description: 50+ farms. 2,000+ customers. Real traction, real risk. Read the Birchal disclosure first.
creative_brief: Lead with radical honesty about investment risk. This builds trust by going against every instinct in advertising. Then stack the real proof points (farms, customers, supply chain). Let the reader decide. Compliance built into the creative, not bolted on.

primary_text:
Most startups fail. We know that.

But the supermarket model is already failing farmers. 50+ regenerative farms lose margin to warehouses and wholesalers. We built FarmThru to go direct. Farm to hub to kitchen.

2,000+ families order from us. They get beef, pork, salmon, chicken and dairy that's days old, not weeks.

Raising on Birchal this July. From $50. Not financial advice. Read the disclosure document before investing.

$5 refundable deposit for VIP access.

creative_brief: Contrarian opener acknowledges the harsh reality of startup investing. Then reframe: the real failure is the existing system that squeezes farmers. Stack traction as evidence (not promises). Full compliance, transparent tone throughout.

sections.heading: The supermarket problem
sections.body: The average supermarket supply chain moves product through 3-4 warehouses over 2-3 weeks. By the time it's on the shelf, the farmer who raised it has already been paid a fraction of what you're paying. You're covering the logistics. The packaging. The shelf space. Not the farming.

sections.heading: We cut the chain to three steps.
sections.body: Farm: 50+ regenerative farms across NSW raise the animals on pasture. Hub: Your order is packed fresh at the Brookvale hub. One stop. No warehouses. Kitchen: On your doorstep in 2-3 days. Sydney, Central Coast, Wollongong.

A paddock. Wild-Caught Salmon: Never farmed. Caught off the coast and delivered before supermarket stock even ships.

subject: You're on the waitlist. Here's what happens next.
preheader: Welcome to FarmThru. Your spot is confirmed and your $5 deposit is fully refundable.
body:
Welcome to FarmThru.

You're now on the VIP waitlist for our equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal, launching July 2026. Your $5 refundable deposit is confirmed.

Here's what that means for you:

1. You get first access when the campaign goes live.
2. You'll receive priority updates from us before anyone else.
3. You're invited to an exclusive founder Q&A before launch.
4. Your deposit is fully refundable at any time, no questions asked.

A bit about us, if you're new. FarmThru delivers regenerative food from 50+ farms across NSW direct to families in Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong. No warehouses. No wholesalers. Paddock to your door in 2-3 days.

2,000+ families already order from us. Now we're opening investment to our community.

Over the next couple of weeks, we'll share more about how FarmThru works, who our farmers are, and what the investment looks like. No pressure. Just information to help you decide.

This is not financial advice. We'll share the Birchal disclosure document when the campaign opens.

Talk soon.

The FarmThru Team

P.S. Questions? Just reply. A real person will answer.
creative_brief: Welcome email. Must land immediately after signup. Confirm the deposit, set expectations for the VIP experience (4 clear benefits), introduce FarmThru briefly for new visitors, and preview the email sequence coming. Warm, no-pressure tone. Reply-friendly.

subject: Why I started FarmThru (the honest version)
preheader: It started with a steak that didn't taste like anything. Then I visited a farm in Kempsey.
body:
I want to tell you why FarmThru exists. The real version, not the polished one.

A few years ago I was cooking dinner for my kids. Steak from the supermarket. It didn't taste like anything. I started wondering how old it was, where it came from, what the animal had eaten.

I couldn't find answers. Not on the packaging. Not on the website. Nobody could tell me.

So I drove up to Kempsey and visited Rachel's Farm. Rachel Ward, the actress turned farmer, had converted her property to regenerative agriculture after the 2020 bushfires. Her cattle grazed open paddocks. No feedlots. No hormones. The land was actually improving.

I bought some beef and brought it home. My kids noticed the difference before I said a word.

That visit changed everything. I started connecting regenerative farms with families who cared about where their food came from. One farm became ten. Ten became fifty. Now FarmThru works with 50+ farms and delivers to 2,000+ households across Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong.

Our model is simple. Farm to our Brookvale hub to your kitchen. No warehouses, no wholesalers. Farmers get a fairer price. You get food that's 2-3 days old, not 14-21 days in cold storage.

We're not perfect. We're still small. But we know what good food tastes like and we know the farmers who grow it.

More soon.

Jeremy
creative_brief: Founder story, day 3. First person from Jeremy. Personal origin moment (cooking for kids, tasteless supermarket steak) to discovery (Rachel's Farm visit) to building FarmThru. Must feel like a genuine letter, not marketing. Vulnerability about being small. No investment CTA in this email.

subject: What 2,000+ families already know about their food
preheader: Real customers, real words. Here's why Sydney families are switching to FarmThru.
body:
We could tell you FarmThru is different. But our customers say it better.

"Finally, a grocery delivery that actually cares about where the food comes from. The quality is incredible." - Sarah, Sydney

"My kids can taste the difference. And I love knowing the farmers are getting a fair price." - James, Northern Beaches

Those aren't made up. They're real people who order from us every week.

Here's the picture right now:

50+ regenerative farms across NSW and beyond. 2,000+ families ordering regularly. Delivery across Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong. Food that arrives 2-3 days from the paddock, not 14-21 days in cold storage.

Every product is 100% sourced from regenerative farms. Beef is grass-fed. Chicken is pasture-raised. Pork is heritage Berkshire from Bundarra. Salmon is wild-caught from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast.

We don't have a marketing budget that can compete with the supermarkets. What we have is food that speaks for itself and customers who keep coming back.

If you haven't tried FarmThru yet, the quality is the best argument we can make.

The FarmThru Team

Not financial advice. Disclosure document on Birchal.
creative_brief: Social proof email, day 6. Lead with verbatim customer testimonials (Sarah, James). Stack traction metrics and product specificity (named farms, named products). Let the social proof do the heavy lifting. No investment CTA yet. Food-as-hero.

subject: Meet the farms behind your food
preheader: Rachel's Farm in Kempsey. Bundarra Berkshires. Collins Wild Caught. Real farms, real names.
body:
One of the things that sets FarmThru apart is that we can tell you exactly where your food comes from. Not a region. Not a brand name. The actual farm.

Here are some of the people we work with:

Rachel's Farm, Kempsey NSW. Rachel Ward runs a regenerative cattle property that she rebuilt after the 2020 bushfires. 100% grass-fed beef, no hormones, no feedlots. Her animals graze open paddocks and the land gets better every year.

Bundarra Berkshires, Bundarra NSW. Heritage Berkshire pork, outdoor-raised. These are old breed pigs raised the way they should be, not in sheds.

Collins Wild Caught, South Coast NSW. Wild-caught Alaskan salmon and seasonal Australian seafood. Not farmed. Not frozen for months.

Paris Creek Farms, Meadows SA. Bio-dynamic dairy from a family farm that has been producing for decades.

Every one of our 50+ partner farms meets the same standard. Regenerative practices. Animals on pasture. No shortcuts.

The supply chain is simple. Farm to our Brookvale hub, then to your door. 2-3 days from paddock to plate, compared to 14-21 days in supermarket cold storage.

That's the difference you can taste.

The FarmThru Team

Not financial advice. Disclosure document on Birchal.
creative_brief: Product deep-dive, day 9. Spotlight named farm partners with specific details (location, product, farming practice). This is the 'know your farmer' email. Educational tone, building trust through specificity. Supply chain comparison at the end. Still no investment CTA.

subject: The investment opportunity (and the risks)
preheader: Equity crowdfunding on Birchal. From $50. Here's what you should know before deciding.
body:
You've heard about the food. Now here's the investment side.

FarmThru is raising via equity crowdfunding on Birchal, launching July 2026. For the first time, everyday Australians can invest in FarmThru alongside the people who already buy from us.

Here are the terms:

Minimum investment: $50. Maximum: $10,000 per investor. The campaign will be hosted on Birchal, Australia's leading equity crowdfunding platform. Your $5 refundable deposit has already secured your VIP access and priority when the campaign opens.

Here's the traction:

50+ regenerative farm partners. 2,000+ regular customers. Delivering across Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong from our Brookvale hub. Direct supply chain with zero warehouses and zero wholesalers.

The Australian grocery market is worth $130B. Direct-to-consumer food delivery is growing at 24% year on year. We believe there's a real opportunity here.

But we also need to be straight with you. Investing in startups carries real risk. Most startups don't succeed. Your investment is not guaranteed and you could lose some or all of your money.

We're not asking you to take our word for it. When the campaign goes live, the Birchal disclosure document will have everything you need to make an informed decision. We'd encourage you to read it carefully and consider seeking independent financial advice.

This is not financial advice.

More soon.

Jeremy
creative_brief: Investment thesis email, day 12. First email that directly addresses the investment opportunity. Clear terms (min/max, platform). Traction stacked as evidence. Market size and growth data. Then mandatory risk disclosure, delivered honestly not as boilerplate. Full ASIC compliance. From Jeremy for personal accountability.

subject: Tomorrow. The FarmThru campaign goes live.
preheader: Your VIP access is confirmed. Here's exactly what to expect when the Birchal campaign opens.
body:
This is the email we've been building towards.

Tomorrow, the FarmThru equity crowdfunding campaign goes live on Birchal.

As a VIP waitlist member, you get first access. Here's what that means:

You'll receive an email tomorrow morning with a direct link to the Birchal campaign page. VIP members get priority access before the campaign opens to the public. Your $5 refundable deposit is still fully refundable if you decide not to invest.

Here's what you'll find on the campaign page:

The full disclosure document with financials, risks and business details. Investment options from $50 to $10,000. The FarmThru story, traction and plans in full.

A few things to remember:

Read the disclosure document before investing. This is a startup investment and carries real risk. Consider seeking independent financial advice if you're unsure. Your $5 deposit is refundable at any time, no questions asked.

We've spent months preparing for this. 50+ regenerative farms. 2,000+ customers. A supply chain that gets real food to your door in days, not weeks.

Now it's up to you to decide if you want to be part of what comes next.

We'll see you tomorrow.

The FarmThru Team
creative_brief: Countdown email, day before launch. Build anticipation with clear logistics (what happens tomorrow, what VIP access means). Remind them the deposit is refundable. Set expectations for the campaign page. Reiterate risk disclosure naturally. Confidence without hype.

subject: It's live. FarmThru is open for investment.
preheader: The Birchal campaign is open. VIP members get priority access right now.
body:
The FarmThru equity crowdfunding campaign is live on Birchal.

You can invest right now.

As a VIP member, you have priority access. Your $5 refundable deposit has been held for this moment.

How to invest:

Click the link below to go to the FarmThru campaign on Birchal. Read the disclosure document. It has everything: financials, risks, the business plan, the team. Choose your investment amount. Minimum $50, maximum $10,000. Complete your investment through Birchal's secure platform.

Why now:

FarmThru delivers regenerative food from 50+ farms to 2,000+ families across Sydney, Central Coast and Wollongong. We've built a direct supply chain that cuts out the warehouses and wholesalers. Food arrives 2-3 days from the paddock. Farmers get a fairer price.

We're growing. And for the first time, the people who eat our food can own a piece of the business that delivers it.

Important:

This is an investment in a startup. It carries real risk including the possibility of losing your entire investment. This is not financial advice. Please read the Birchal disclosure document in full and consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.

If you decide not to invest, your $5 deposit is fully refundable.

Thank you for being here from the beginning.

Jeremy
creative_brief: Launch day email. Campaign is LIVE. Clear how-to-invest steps (link, read disclosure, choose amount, complete). Restate the business case briefly. Strong compliance paragraph with explicit risk language. From Jeremy for personal weight. Gratitude close.
